laws.title: Ohio Minimum Wage — $11.00/hr (Effective January 1, 2026)
laws.summary: Ohio's minimum wage increased to $11.00 per hour effective January 1, 2026, for employers with annual gross receipts exceeding $405,000. Employers at or below that threshold must pay the federal minimum of $7.25/hour. Ohio adjusts its minimum wage annually based on the Consumer Price Index.
laws.employee_threshold_note: $11.00/hr applies to employers with annual gross receipts over $405,000; those at or below must pay $7.25/hr
compliance_actions: Determine whether your annual gross receipts exceed $405,000 to confirm your applicable rate
Update payroll to $11.00/hour (or $7.25 for smaller employers) effective January 1, 2026
For tipped employees at $11.00/hr employers: ensure employer-paid wage ($5.50 minimum) plus tips equals at least $11.00/hour
Post the updated 2026 Ohio Minimum Wage poster — a new poster is required each January
